summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes attacker-controlled binary, then erases itself
details: Package ships a non-functional email-confirmation API (index.js exports `sendConfirmationEmail` and `verifyToken` that only console.log and `return true`) as a cover story for a postinstall dropper. `package.json` declares `postinstall: node install-hook.js`. install-hook.js base64-decodes an embedded blob, XORs each byte with 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%/tmp.exe` (or `/tmp/tmp.exe`), and executes it detached with `windowsHide: true`. The decoded payload is a UTF-16LE PowerShell loader that fetches https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XOR-decrypts it with the same 0x42 key, writes to `%TEMP%/tmp.exe`, and Start-Process launches it hidden — delivering attacker-controlled code to the installer's machine on `npm install`. The hook then writes `%TEMP%/cleanup_<ts>.js`, spawns it detached via `node`, and after a 60-second delay removes the `mailconfirmer` entry from the project's `package.json` and `package-lock.json` and recursively deletes the module directory — anti-forensics to hide the package after the payload has executed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
